2603.11342.title: Evaluating Explainable AI Attribution Methods in Neural Machine Translation via Attention-Guided Knowledge Distillation
2603.11342.paper_summary: The study of the attribution of input features to the output of neural network models is an active area of research. While numerous Explainable AI (XAI) techniques have been proposed to interpret these models, the systematic and automated evaluation of these methods in sequence-to-sequence (seq2seq) models is less explored. This paper introduces a new approach for evaluating explainability methods in transformer-based seq2seq models. We use teacher-derived attribution maps as a structured side signal to guide a student model, and quantify the utility of different attribution methods through the student's ability to simulate targets. Using the Inseq library, we extract attribution scores over source-target sequence pairs and inject these scores into the attention mechanism of a student transformer model under four composition operators (addition, multiplication, averaging, and replacement). Across three language pairs (de-en, fr-en, ar-en) and attributions from Marian-MT and mBART models, Attention, Value Zeroing, and Layer Gradient $\times$ Activation consistently yield the largest gains in BLEU (and corresponding improvements in chrF) relative to baselines. In contrast, other gradient-based methods (Saliency, Integrated Gradients, DeepLIFT, Input $\times$ Gradient, GradientShap) lead to smaller and less consistent improvements. These results suggest that different attribution methods capture distinct signals and that attention-derived attributions better capture alignment between source and target representations in seq2seq models. Finally, we introduce an Attributor transformer that, given a source-target pair, learns to reconstruct the teacher's attribution map. Our findings demonstrate that the more accurately the Attributor can reproduce attribution maps, the more useful an injection of those maps is for the downstream task. The source code can be found on GitHub.
2603.11342.paper_summary_zh: 神經網絡模型的輸入特徵與輸出之間的歸因研究是一個活躍的研究領域。雖然已提出許多可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）技術來解釋這些模型，但對於這些方法在序列到序列（seq2seq）模型中的系統性和自動化評估則較少被探索。本文介紹了一種新的方法，用於評估基於Transformer的seq2seq模型中的可解釋性方法。我們使用教師衍生的歸因圖作為結構化的側信號來指導學生模型，並通過學生模擬目標的能力來量化不同歸因方法的效用。使用Inseq庫，我們提取源-目標序列對的歸因分數，並將這些分數注入到學生Transformer模型的注意力機制中，使用四種組合運算符（加法、乘法、平均和替換）。在三對語言（德英、法英、阿英）和來自Marian-MT及mBART模型的歸因中，注意力、值歸零和層梯度 $\times$ 激活相對於基準始終產生最大的BLEU增益（以及相應的chrF改善）。相比之下，其他基於梯度的方法（顯著性、積分梯度、DeepLIFT、輸入 $\times$ 梯度、GradientShap）則導致較小且不一致的改善。這些結果表明，不同的歸因方法捕捉到不同的信號，而基於注意力的歸因更好地捕捉到seq2seq模型中源和目標表示之間的對齊。最後，我們介紹了一種AttributorTransformer，給定一對源-目標，學習重建教師的歸因圖。我們的研究結果顯示，Attributor能夠更準確地重現歸因圖時，這些圖的注入對下游任務的幫助更大。源代碼可以在GitHub上找到。
2603.11342.author: Aria Nourbakhsh et.al.
2603.11342.authors: Aria Nourbakhsh, Salima Lamsiyah, Adelaide Danilov, Christoph Schommer
